ICRA 2021 Reading List

  • Reading List
Jun 03, 2021

This is my personal post-ICRA 2021 reading list with papers I came across while attending the conference and which I particularly want to read. It is also intended as a resource for my colleagues who did not attend ICRA this year.

Most papers will be about task- or motion-level robot learning, but many intersect with other domains as well. If you presented a paper at ICRA you think I would like and it is not on this list, please send me an email and I promise to read it!

Highlighted are papers I read (or attended the presentation) and found especially insightful. This list is subject to change as I read my way through it or add more from the proceedings.

  1. T. Kulak, H. Girgin, J.-M. Odobez, and S. Calinon, “Active Learning of Bayesian Probabilistic Movement Primitives,” IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters, vol. 6, no. 2, pp. 2163–2170, Apr. 2021, doi: 10.1109/LRA.2021.3060414.
  2. Y. Du, O. Watkins, T. Darrell, P. Abbeel, and D. Pathak, “Auto-Tuned Sim-to-Real Transfer,” arXiv:2104.07662 [cs], May 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2104.07662. Link
  3. J.-S. Ha, Y.-J. Park, H.-J. Chae, S.-S. Park, and H.-L. Choi, “Distilling a Hierarchical Policy for Planning and Control via Representation and Reinforcement Learning,” arXiv:2011.08345 [cs], Apr. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2011.08345. Link
  4. M. Lechner, R. Hasani, R. Grosu, D. Rus, and T. A. Henzinger, “Adversarial Training is Not Ready for Robot Learning,” arXiv:2103.08187 [cs], Mar. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2103.08187. Link
  5. S. Li, D. Park, Y. Sung, J. A. Shah, and N. Roy, “Reactive Task and Motion Planning under Temporal Logic Specifications,” arXiv:2103.14464 [cs], Mar. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2103.14464. Link
  6. A. Sidiropoulos, Y. Karayiannidis, and Z. Doulgeri, “Human-robot collaborative object transfer using human motion prediction based on Cartesian pose Dynamic Movement Primitives,” arXiv:2104.03155 [cs], Apr. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2104.03155. Link
  7. A. S. Sathya, G. Pipeleers, W. Decré, and J. Swevers, “A Weighted Method for Fast Resolution of Strictly Hierarchical Robot Task Specifications Using Exact Penalty Functions,” IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters, vol. 6, no. 2, pp. 3057–3064, Apr. 2021, doi: 10.1109/LRA.2021.3063026.
  8. S. Malla, C. Choi, and B. Dariush, “Social-STAGE: Spatio-Temporal Multi-Modal Future Trajectory Forecast,” arXiv:2011.04853 [cs], Mar. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2011.04853. Link
  9. S. Tosatto, G. Chalvatzaki, and J. Peters, “Contextual Latent-Movements Off-Policy Optimization for Robotic Manipulation Skills,” arXiv:2010.13766 [cs], May 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2010.13766. Link
  10. E. Johns, “Coarse-to-Fine Imitation Learning: Robot Manipulation from a Single Demonstration,” arXiv:2105.06411 [cs], May 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2105.06411. Link
  11. M. Sundermeyer, A. Mousavian, R. Triebel, and D. Fox, “Contact-GraspNet: Efficient 6-DoF Grasp Generation in Cluttered Scenes,” arXiv:2103.14127 [cs], Mar. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2103.14127. Link
  12. K. Boggess, S. Chen, and L. Feng, “Towards Personalized Explanation of Robot Path Planning via User Feedback,” arXiv:2011.00524 [cs], Mar. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2011.00524. Link
  13. T. Power and D. Berenson, “Keep it Simple: Data-efficient Learning for Controlling Complex Systems with Simple Models,” arXiv:2102.02493 [cs], Feb. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2102.02493. Link
  14. T. Dinev, W. Merkt, V. Ivan, I. Havoutis, and S. Vijayakumar, “Sparsity-Inducing Optimal Control via Differential Dynamic Programming,” arXiv:2011.07325 [cs], Mar. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2011.07325. Link
  15. S. Luo, H. Kasaei, and L. Schomaker, “Self-Imitation Learning by Planning,” arXiv:2103.13834 [cs], Mar. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2103.13834. Link
  16. È. Pairet, C. Chamzas, Y. Petillot, and L. E. Kavraki, “Path Planning for Manipulation using Experience-driven Random Trees,” IEEE Robot. Autom. Lett., vol. 6, no. 2, pp. 3295–3302, Apr. 2021, doi: 10.1109/LRA.2021.3063063. Link
  17. T. E. Lee, J. Zhao, A. S. Sawhney, S. Girdhar, and O. Kroemer, “Causal Reasoning in Simulation for Structure and Transfer Learning of Robot Manipulation Policies,” arXiv:2103.16772 [cs], Mar. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2103.16772. Link
  18. M. Wulfmeier et al., “Representation Matters: Improving Perception and Exploration for Robotics,” arXiv:2011.01758 [cs, stat], Mar. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2011.01758. Link
  19. A. Allshire, R. Martín-Martín, C. Lin, S. Manuel, S. Savarese, and A. Garg, “LASER: Learning a Latent Action Space for Efficient Reinforcement Learning,” arXiv:2103.15793 [cs], Mar. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2103.15793. Link
  20. J. Liu, W. Zeng, R. Urtasun, and E. Yumer, “Deep Structured Reactive Planning,” arXiv:2101.06832 [cs], Apr. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2101.06832. Link
  21. A. S. Chen, H. J. Nam, S. Nair, and C. Finn, “Batch Exploration with Examples for Scalable Robotic Reinforcement Learning,” IEEE Robot. Autom. Lett., vol. 6, no. 3, pp. 4401–4408, Jul. 2021, doi: 10.1109/LRA.2021.3068655. Link
  22. A. S. Morgan, D. Nandha, G. Chalvatzaki, C. D’Eramo, A. M. Dollar, and J. Peters, “Model Predictive Actor-Critic: Accelerating Robot Skill Acquisition with Deep Reinforcement Learning,” arXiv:2103.13842 [cs], Mar. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2103.13842. Link
  23. H. Wen, X. Chen, G. Papagiannis, C. Hu, and Y. Li, “End-To-End Semi-supervised Learning for Differentiable Particle Filters,” arXiv:2011.05748 [cs, stat], Mar. 2021, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2011.05748. Link
  24. S. Bechtle, B. Hammoud, A. Rai, F. Meier, and L. Righetti, “Leveraging Forward Model Prediction Error for Learning Control,” arXiv:2011.03859 [cs], Nov. 2020, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2011.03859. Link
  25. Y. Wu, M. Mozifian, and F. Shkurti, “Shaping Rewards for Reinforcement Learning with Imperfect Demonstrations using Generative Models,” arXiv:2011.01298 [cs], Nov. 2020, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2011.01298. Link
  26. A. Haidu and M. Beetz, “Automated acquisition of structured, semantic models of manipulation activities from human VR demonstration,” arXiv:2011.13689 [cs], Nov. 2020, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2011.13689. Link
  27. C. Cioflan and R. Timofte, “MS-RANAS: Multi-Scale Resource-Aware Neural Architecture Search,” arXiv:2009.13940 [cs], Sep. 2020, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2009.13940. Link
  28. M. T. Akbulut, U. Bozdogan, A. Tekden, and E. Ugur, “Reward Conditioned Neural Movement Primitives for Population Based Variational Policy Optimization,” arXiv:2011.04282 [cs], Nov. 2020, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2011.04282. Link
  29. M. Lutter, J. Silberbauer, J. Watson, and J. Peters, “Differentiable Physics Models for Real-world Offline Model-based Reinforcement Learning,” arXiv:2011.01734 [cs], Nov. 2020, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2011.01734. Link
  30. D. Ho, K. Rao, Z. Xu, E. Jang, M. Khansari, and Y. Bai, “RetinaGAN: An Object-aware Approach to Sim-to-Real Transfer,” arXiv:2011.03148 [cs], Nov. 2020, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2011.03148. Link
  31. G. P. Meyer et al., “LaserFlow: Efficient and Probabilistic Object Detection and Motion Forecasting,” arXiv:2003.05982 [cs], Oct. 2020, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/2003.05982. Link
  32. Y. Lee, E. S. Hu, Z. Yang, A. Yin, and J. J. Lim, “IKEA Furniture Assembly Environment for Long-Horizon Complex Manipulation Tasks,” arXiv:1911.07246 [cs], Nov. 2019, [Online]. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/1911.07246. Link
  33. D. Driess, J.-S. Ha, R. Tedrake, and M. Toussaint, “Learning Geometric Reasoning and Control for Long-Horizon Tasks from Visual Input,” p. 8.
